vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1191: Create workspace docker stack on Start (not Plan), with progress dialog

plan.title: Create workspace docker stack on Start (not Plan), with progress dialog
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Workspace docker stacks used to be provisioned during planning. That coupling was removed but the corresponding provision-on-Start work never landed. The result: clicking dashboard Start on any issue without an existing stack returns 422 'No Docker containers found for workspace stack', forcing the user to choose between two bad workarounds — `pan workspace rebuild` from the CLI or `pan start --host` (bypasses container isolation). PAN-1148, PAN-1169, PAN-1189, and PAN-1190 all hit this during the /all-up flywheel run on 2026-05-18.
narratives.Proposal: Introduce a shared `provisionWorkspaceStack()` helper in `src/lib/workspace/provision-stack.ts` that re-renders `.devcontainer/` if missing, runs `docker compose up -d --build`, and polls `getWorkspaceStackHealth()` until the stack reports healthy — all behind a per-issue mutex and a global concurrency cap of 2. Add a new SSE endpoint `POST /api/agents/start-stream` that mirrors the existing `/api/issues/:id/start-planning` pattern and emits milestone-level progress events (worktree, .devcontainer, compose up, health, agent spawn). The dashboard Start button opens a `StartAgentDialog` that consumes the SSE stream and renders a checklist with elapsed time per step, plus Retry / Force rebuild / Switch to --host (advanced) on failure. The CLI `pan start` calls the same helper and prints a multi-line ticker to stdout. The existing non-streaming `POST /api/agents` route also invokes the helper so Cloister/host-override paths self-heal too. Healthy stacks short-circuit with no compose call. The `--host` break-glass continues to skip provisioning entirely.

autoDecisions.summary: SSE over a new endpoint (not Effect RPC stream, not modifying the existing /api/agents POST).
autoDecisions.rationale: Mirrors the established `/api/issues/:id/start-planning` precedent in the codebase; PlanDialog already proves the streaming-body-fetch pattern works in this frontend; one-way only; doesn't disturb existing /api/agents callers (Cloister, host override retries).

autoDecisions.summary: Milestone progress events only (worktree → .devcontainer → compose up → health → spawn); no docker compose log tailing in phase 1.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the checklist shape in the issue body. Compose-log tailing is a real UX upgrade but a strictly additive follow-up that should not block the core fix.

autoDecisions.summary: Provisioning helper is invoked from Start paths (dashboard SSE, dashboard non-streaming POST, CLI pan start) but NOT from `assertWorkspaceStackHealthyForSpawn()` (which is also called by review/test/ship roles and Cloister auto-resume).
autoDecisions.rationale: Review/test/ship run on the host and shouldn't trigger long-running docker provisioning. Cloister auto-resume is unattended and should surface the unhealthy state through the dashboard rather than silently rebuild — that's a separate Cloister UX decision.

autoDecisions.summary: Per-issue async mutex + global concurrency cap of 2 simultaneous compose-up runs; further Starts queue.
autoDecisions.rationale: Risk #1 in the issue body explicitly calls out concurrent stack-up storms. /all-up routinely triggers many Starts in parallel. 2 is conservative; can be raised later if it becomes the bottleneck.

autoDecisions.summary: `provisionWorkspaceStack` short-circuits when `getWorkspaceStackHealth()` already returns healthy (emits 'stack-already-healthy' progress event, returns immediately).
autoDecisions.rationale: Required by the acceptance criterion 'Pre-existing healthy stacks skip the provision step (idempotent)'.

autoDecisions.summary: All new server-reachable code uses async I/O (fs/promises, execAsync). Existing sync calls in workspaceRebuildCommand are migrated as part of the extraction.
autoDecisions.rationale: Once the helper is reachable from the dashboard route, sync I/O blocks the Node event loop (PAN-70 / PAN-446 lineage). Project rule `no-execsync-server.md` is explicit.

autoDecisions.summary: PAN-1169 is recorded as a hard prerequisite. The work agent rebases onto main after PAN-1169 merges before final UAT.
autoDecisions.rationale: PAN-1169 fixes bun --filter not populating top-level node_modules during init. Without it, every compose up in this provisioning path fails at the bun-install phase. Issue body 'Related' section calls this out explicitly.

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/workspace/provision-stack.ts` exporting `provisionWorkspaceStack(issueId, options)` with an `onProgress` callback. The function: (1) resolves project + workspacePath via existing `resolveProjectFromIssue`/`getProject` helpers, (2) calls `getWorkspaceStackHealth()` first — if healthy, emits a `stack-already-healthy` progress event and returns, (3) otherwise calls async `ensureDevcontainerAsync()` (new fs/promises sibling of `ensureDevcontainer`), (4) optionally runs `docker compose down -v --remove-orphans` when `options.force === true`, (5) runs `docker compose up -d --build` via execAsync with a 300s timeout, (6) polls `getWorkspaceStackHealth()` every 2s up to 120s until healthy, emitting heartbeat progress events every 10s during long phases. Returns `{ success, stackHealth, durations }`. NO sync fs calls, NO execSync.

subItems.title: `provisionWorkspaceStack()` exists at `src/lib/workspace/provision-stack.ts` and exports a single async function with the documented signature.
When `getWorkspaceStackHealth()` returns healthy on entry, the function emits exactly one `stack-already-healthy` progress event and returns without invoking `docker compose`.
When the stack is missing, the function calls the canonical `renderDevcontainer()` via an async wrapper (no duplicate template rendering logic).
All filesystem and process I/O uses fs/promises + execAsync. `grep -nE 'execSync|readFileSync|writeFileSync|rmSync|readdirSync|statSync' src/lib/workspace/provision-stack.ts` returns no matches.
Heartbeat progress events fire every ~10s during compose-up and health-poll phases, so an idle SSE client doesn't see a quiet stream during long provisioning.
Health-poll terminates with a clear error after 120s of consecutive unhealthy reads; error includes the latest `stackHealth.reasons`.
`renderDevcontainer()` no-mount + `PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1` invariants are preserved (no new mount of `~/.panopticon` is introduced anywhere in this module).

items.title: Add per-issue mutex + global concurrency cap of 2 for workspace provisioning

narrative.Action: Add `withProvisioningLock(issueId, fn)` in `src/lib/workspace/provisioning-lock.ts`. Per-issue: chain promises by normalized issueId so a duplicate Start for the same issue waits for the in-flight call. Global: a 2-slot async semaphore around the inner work. Mutex released in try/finally. Used by `provisionWorkspaceStack()`.

subItems.title: Two concurrent `provisionWorkspaceStack('PAN-X', ...)` calls for the same issue serialize — the second receives the first's resolved value rather than re-running.
Five concurrent `provisionWorkspaceStack()` calls for five different issues run at most 2 at a time; the other 3 queue.
When the inner function throws, the mutex slot is released (next caller can proceed without timing out).

items.title: Refactor CLI `workspaceRebuildCommand` to delegate to `provisionWorkspaceStack({ force: true })`

narrative.Action: Strip the docker-compose-down/up/render duplicate logic out of `src/cli/commands/workspace-rebuild.ts` and replace with a call to `provisionWorkspaceStack(issueId, { force: true, onProgress: cliRenderer })`. Keep the ora spinner, but drive it from the helper's progress events. Preserve `composeProjectNameForWorkspace()` (the guard) and the existing unit tests.

subItems.title: `pan workspace rebuild <id>` still rebuilds the stack end-to-end on a corrupted workspace.
The COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME guard test in `src/cli/commands/__tests__/workspace-rebuild.test.ts` continues to pass.
`workspace-rebuild.ts` no longer contains its own `docker compose` exec calls — they live only in `provision-stack.ts`.

narrative.Action: Add a new HttpRouter.add route in `src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts` (or a new sibling file `agents-start-stream.ts` registered from `server.ts`). The route: (1) performs the same pre-flight validation as POST /api/agents (origin, issueId parse, host-override confirmation, beads, guardrails, codex/provider health) and returns JSON 4xx errors before the stream opens; (2) opens an SSE ReadableStream with `Content-Type: text/event-stream`; (3) emits `started` event with issue + workspace metadata; (4) calls `provisionWorkspaceStack()` with an onProgress that forwards `progress` events; (5) on success, runs the existing spawn flow and emits a `spawning` and final `complete` event with `{ agentId, sessionName }`; (6) on failure, emits a single `error` event with `{ error, hint, kind }` then closes.

subItems.title: POSTing to `/api/agents/start-stream` with `{issueId, role: 'work', model, harness}` returns `Content-Type: text/event-stream` and streams `data: {...}\n\n` events.
Pre-stream JSON errors (origin block, codex burned, beads missing, guardrails block) are returned with the same status codes and shapes as POST /api/agents.
Successful provisioning emits at minimum: started → progress(worktree) → progress(devcontainer) → progress(compose) → progress(health) → spawning → complete.

On provisioning failure, the stream emits exactly one `error` event with `{ error, hint, kind: 'provision_failed' | 'spawn_failed' | 'unhealthy_after_timeout' }` then closes cleanly (no half-open SSE).
The route is wired into the Effect HTTP server via the existing `httpHandler` pattern and uses async I/O exclusively.

narrative.Action: In the existing POST /api/agents flow in `src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts`, immediately before the stack-health gate (around the existing `getWorkspaceStackHealth` call), call `provisionWorkspaceStack(issueId, { force: false })` when `!allowHost && !isRemote`. Skip when `allowHost` is true (host override). No progress streaming on this path — just synchronous (awaited) provisioning before the gate. This keeps non-dashboard callers (host-override retries, Cloister auto-resume's manual restart trigger, CLI fallback) working without re-introducing the regression.

subItems.title: POSTing `/api/agents` for a non-existent-stack issue without `--host` brings the stack up and then spawns the agent (no longer returns 422 'No Docker containers found').
POSTing `/api/agents` with `host: true` + confirmation still skips provisioning entirely and falls through to the existing host-override path.
If `provisionWorkspaceStack()` throws, the route returns 422 with the same shape it already returns for unhealthy-stack failures (`error`, `hint`, `stackHealth`).

items.title: Make `pan start <id>` provision the stack before spawn (when not --host)

narrative.Action: In `src/cli/commands/start.ts`, between workspace resolution and the spawn call, invoke `provisionWorkspaceStack(issueId, { force: false, onProgress: cliRenderer })` when `!options.host`. The CLI renderer prints a multi-line checklist (one `✓`/`⟳`/`✗` line per step with elapsed time), matching the issue body's expected output. `--host --yes` continues to skip provisioning entirely.

subItems.title: `pan start PAN-X` (no `--host`) on an issue with a missing stack prints the multi-line checklist and successfully starts the agent.
`pan start PAN-X --host --yes` skips the provisioning checklist and falls through to the existing host-override start path.
`pan start PAN-X` on an issue with an already-healthy stack prints a single `✓ Workspace stack already healthy — skipping provision` line and proceeds to spawn.

items.title: Add `StartAgentDialog` frontend component consuming the SSE stream

narrative.Action: Add `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/StartAgentDialog.tsx` modelled on PlanDialog's `setting-up` step. It opens when KanbanBoard's Start mutation is invoked, owns a fetch-with-streaming-body call to `/api/agents/start-stream`, parses SSE events with the same `data: ` line-splitter PlanDialog uses, renders a checklist (one row per progress step with status icon, label, detail, and elapsed time), and closes itself when it receives the final `complete` event. Wire it into `KanbanBoard.tsx` so the Start button opens the dialog (replacing the silent fire-and-forget mutation for the dashboard path). The auto-start button (Start without planning) opens the same dialog with `auto: true`.

subItems.title: `StartAgentDialog` renders a modal with a vertical checklist of steps, status icon, label, detail line, and elapsed seconds per step.
The dialog opens when the Start button on a kanban card is clicked and stays open until the SSE stream emits `complete` or `error`.
On `complete`, the dialog closes and the dashboard transitions to the agent's terminal pane (existing behavior after start succeeds).
Pre-stream HTTP errors (non-200 response) render the error message + hint inline in the dialog with a Retry button — matching PlanDialog's error UX.
Auto-start button (existing `card-auto-start-agent-*`) opens the same dialog with `auto: true` in the request body so a fresh vBRIEF is synthesized inline.

items.title: Wire Retry / Force rebuild / Switch to --host (advanced) recovery actions in the dialog

narrative.Action: On failure, render three buttons under the error: (1) 'Retry' re-runs the same SSE request unchanged; (2) 'Force rebuild' re-runs with `{ force: true }` in the request body, which makes `provisionWorkspaceStack({ force: true })` do a `compose down -v` + re-render first; (3) 'Switch to --host (advanced)' triggers the existing two-step host-override flow against the non-streaming POST /api/agents endpoint. The third button is visually de-emphasized (smaller text, muted color) to discourage casual use.

subItems.title: Clicking 'Retry' re-opens the SSE stream with the same body (no force) and replaces the failed step list.
Clicking 'Force rebuild' sends `{ force: true }`, the helper runs `compose down -v --remove-orphans` then re-renders `.devcontainer/` then brings the stack up.

Clicking 'Switch to --host (advanced)' walks the user through the existing hostOverrideConfirmation prompt and then issues the non-streaming POST /api/agents with the host flag; the dialog reports success/failure inline.
The three buttons are visible only when the dialog is in an error state, not during normal progress.

items.title: Add unit + integration tests for the provisioning helper, mutex, SSE route, and dialog

narrative.Action: Unit tests in `src/lib/workspace/__tests__/provision-stack.test.ts` covering: healthy short-circuit, missing-devcontainer render, compose-up failure path, health-poll timeout, force=true compose-down. Mutex tests in `src/lib/workspace/__tests__/provisioning-lock.test.ts` covering per-issue serialization, global cap, error-path release. SSE route integration test in `src/dashboard/server/__tests__/agents-start-stream.test.ts` asserting the event sequence and pre-stream error contracts. Frontend test in `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/__tests__/StartAgentDialog.test.tsx` for the checklist render shape.

subItems.title: `npm test` passes with the new test files in place.
Provision-stack tests cover the four scenarios listed in the Action: healthy short-circuit, render-then-up, compose-up failure, health-poll timeout.
SSE route test asserts the exact event-type sequence for a success path and the error event shape for a forced failure.

items.title: Update WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md + run end-to-end UAT against PAN-1148/1169/1189/1190 fixtures

narrative.Action: Add a 'Provisioning on Start' section to `docs/WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md` documenting the new flow (helper, SSE endpoint, dialog, CLI behavior, the --host break-glass, the Cloister auto-resume gap as a known follow-up). Drive a Playwright UAT (isolated browser instance) against the dashboard: click Start on a test issue with an absent stack, walk through the dialog, observe the agent terminal pane after success. Confirm the four issues named in the acceptance criteria (PAN-1148, PAN-1169, PAN-1189, PAN-1190) can all start without --host fallback after this lands (verify against current main once PAN-1169 has merged).

subItems.title: `docs/WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md` has a new 'Provisioning on Start' section describing the helper, SSE endpoint, dialog, CLI behavior, and the Cloister auto-resume known gap.

Playwright UAT script (`tests/e2e/start-agent-provisioning.spec.ts` or equivalent existing location) clicks Start on a fresh issue, observes the dialog steps, and asserts the agent terminal becomes visible. Uses an isolated browser instance/profile.
Manual confirmation recorded in the PR description that PAN-1148, PAN-1169, PAN-1189, and PAN-1190 can each be started from the dashboard without the --host fallback after this lands.
